name: Interior partition wall
description: Thin (1-block) interior wall that divides two rooms inside a building. Unlike a perimeter wall it has no stone foundation course and no exterior cornice — the partition sits on whatever floor the host room already provides. Includes an optional 1-wide × 2-tall door opening at the centre of the wall and an optional single-block glass pane above the door for borrowed light between rooms.

required_furniture.rule: Solid 1-block-thick fill of @primary along the longer horizontal axis of the AABB.
1-wide × 2-tall gap (minecraft:cave_air) at the centre of the wall axis; the composer drops the air so the gap becomes a real doorway. Suppress with kwarg door_opening_at_center=False.
Single @glass pane one block above the door head, only emitted when the wall has enough vertical clearance and kwarg window_above_door=True.

placement_rules: Wall runs along the longer of (W, D); thin axis is collapsed to the AABB's midline so the partition sits in the middle of the host room.
Never use stone (@secondary) at the base — the partition is interior; the room's floor remains visible underneath.
lighting_ventilation: Partition is interior so it brings no exterior light of its own. The optional glass pane above the door provides borrowed light between adjoining rooms; the doorway itself is the main air path.

medieval.notes: Oak-plank partition between rooms; clear glass borrowed-light over the door.

modern.notes: Smooth-quartz partition reads as drywall; white stained-glass transom for soft light.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak partition with purple stained-glass transom above the door.

python_skill_signature: build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
